articles.title: The porosity test is broken — here's what actually matters
articles.snip: The float test measures surface damage, not how your hair absorbs moisture. UK chemists say skip it. Here is the 30-second behaviour check that actually tells you something.

articles.title: How to read your hair porosity: the full guide
articles.snip: What low, medium, and high porosity actually mean for natural and Afro hair in the UK — and how to adjust your routine accordingly.

articles.title: Why your products stopped working in autumn
articles.snip: UK indoor heating drops humidity to 30–40%. Your glycerin-heavy products are working against you.

latest_articles.headline: The body of work, growing

editorial_signature.sample_label: A sample paragraph, from the archive
editorial_signature.sample_text: Hard water leaves mineral build-up that blocks moisture absorption — it is not your products failing, it is your water. The standard first move is a chelating shampoo once a month. You will know it has worked when your hair stops feeling coated within a day of washing. If it does not, the next thing to look at is protein overload, which feels almost identical.
editorial_signature.tagline: Hair advice with nothing to sell you.
